title: Intensely Sweet Tunisian Lamb Tagine
description: A yeasty, gooey, comforting Tunisian dish featuring sweet smoked paprika, this tagine is perfect for a cold evening, and is garlic and onion free.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the lamb pieces and brown on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the sweet smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the chicken broth, honey, dried apricots, and prunes, and stir to combine.
Cover the Dutch oven and transfer to the preheated oven.
Bake for 2.5 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce is thick and syrupy.
About 30 minutes before serving, stir in the active dry yeast.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
Cook poultry to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F before serving.
